metadata.name: libmozdata
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL 2.0)
metadata.summary: Library to access and aggregate several Mozilla data sources.

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_best
documentation:
Get the key which has the higher value

Args:
    stats (dict): the stats contained in a dictionary

Returns:
    a key

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_timestamp
documentation:
Get a timestamp from a date

Args:
    dt: a string or a datetime object

Returns:
    int: the corresponding timestamp

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_date_from_timestamp
documentation:
Get a UTC date from a timestamp

Args:
    ts(int): a timestamp

Returns:
    datetime.datetime: the corresponding datetime in UTC tz

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_date_ymd
documentation:
Get a datetime from a string 'Year-month-day'

Args:
    dt (str): a date

Returns:
    datetime: a datetime object

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_today
documentation:
Get the date for today

Returns:
    str: the date of today

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_date_str
documentation:
Get the date as string

Args:
    ymd (datetime): a datetime
Returns:
    str: the date as a string 'Year-month-day'

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_date
documentation:
Get the date as string

Args:
    ymd (str): a date
    delta (int): the number of days to remove
Returns:
    str: the date as a string 'Year-month-day'

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_now_timestamp
documentation:
Get timestamp for now

Returns:
    int: timestamp for now

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.is64
documentation:
Check if a cpu is 64 bits or not

Args:
    cpu_name (str): the cpu name

Returns:
    bool: True if 64 is in the name

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.percent
documentation:
Get a percent from a ratio (0.23 => 23%)

Args:
    x (float): ratio

Returns:
    str: a string with a percent

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.simple_percent
documentation:
Get a percent string

Args:
    x (float): number

Returns:
    str: a string with a percent

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_sample
documentation:
Get a random sample from the data according to the fraction

Args:
    data (list): data
    fraction (float): the fraction

Returns:
    list: a random sample

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_date_from_buildid
documentation:
Get a date from buildid

Args:
    bid (str): build_id

Returns:
    date: date object

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_buildid_from_date
documentation:
Get a buildid from a date

Args:
    d (datetime.datetime): the date

Returns:
    str: the build_id

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.as_utc
documentation:
Convert a date in UTC

Args:
    d (datetime.datetime): the date

Returns:
    datetime.datetime: the localized date

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.get_moz_date
documentation:
Convert a Mozilla date in UTC

Args:
    d (str): the date

Returns:
    datetime.datetime: the localized date

functions.name: libmozdata.utils.rate
documentation:
Compute a rate

Args:
    x (num): numerator
    y (num): denominator

Returns:
    float: x / y or Nan if y == 0

from a filename

methods.name: libmozdata.BZInfo.BZInfo.get_best_collaborator
documentation:
Get the 'best' collaborator

A collaboration between A & B is when A reviews a patch of B (or reciprocally)
in term of graph:
   - each node represents a reviewer or a writer (owner)
   - each edge represents a collaboration
here we count the degree of each node and find out who's the best collaborator

it could be interesting to weight each contribution according to its date
someone who made 20 contribs recently is probably better than someone 50 contribs
two years ago...

Returns:
    (str): a collaborator

methods.name: libmozdata.BZInfo.BZInfo.get_best_component_product
documentation:
Get stats on components and products

The idea is to be able to get the component and the product where this file lives.
Useful to prefile bug report

Returns:
    (tuple): a pair containing 'best' component & product

methods.name: libmozdata.redash.Redash.get_khours
documentation:
Get the results for query 346, 387: https://sql.telemetry.mozilla.org/queries/346
                                       https://sql.telemetry.mozilla.org/queries/387
Args:
    start_date (datetime.datetime): start date
    end_date (datetime.datetime): end date
    channel (str): the channel
    versions (List[str]): the versions
    product (str): the product

Returns:
    dict: containing result in json for each query

methods.name: libmozdata.redash.Redash.get_number_of_crash
documentation:
Get the results for query 399, 400: https://sql.telemetry.mozilla.org/queries/399
                                       https://sql.telemetry.mozilla.org/queries/400
Args:
    start_date (datetime.datetime): start date
    end_date (datetime.datetime): end date
    channel (str): the channel
    versions (List[str]): the versions
    product (str): the product

Returns:
    dict: containing result in json for each query
re:dash connection: https://sql.telemetry.mozilla.org

Load a stack of patches with related commits, to reach a given Phabricator Diff

methods.name: libmozdata.bugzilla.Bugzilla.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Args:
    bugids (List[str]): list of bug ids or search query
    include_fields (List[str]): list of include fields
    bughandler (Optional[function]): the handler to use with each retrieved bug
    bugdata (Optional): the data to use with the bug handler
    historyhandler (Optional[function]): the handler to use with each retrieved bug history
    historydata (Optional): the data to use with the history handler
    commenthandler (Optional[function]): the handler to use with each retrieved bug comment
    commentdata (Optional): the data to use with the comment handler
    comment_include_fields (Optional[List[str]]): list of comment include fields
    attachmentids (List[str]): list of attachment ids to retrieve
    attachmenthandler (Optional[function]): the handler to use with each retrieved bug attachment
    attachmentdata (Optional): the data to use with the attachment handler
    attachment_include_fields (Optional[List[str]]): list of attachment include fields
    queries (List[Query]): queries rather than single query

methods.name: libmozdata.bugzilla.Bugzilla.follow_dup
documentation:
Follow the duplicated bugs

Args:
    bugids (List[str]): list of bug ids
    only_final (bool): if True only the final bug is returned else all the chain

Returns:
    dict: each bug in entry is mapped to the last bug in the duplicate chain (None if there's no dup and 'cycle' if a cycle is detected)
